description: Structured comparison of HELIX benchmark results: PC vs Jetson Orin NX

detection_latency.metric: Time to detect anomaly after spike injection

jetson_vs_pc_ratio.note: Jetson 36% slower on mean detection latency (0.049 vs 0.036 ms)

throughput.metric: Samples processed per second (100k sample run)

jetson_vs_pc_ratio.note: Jetson achieves 64% of PC throughput. Still 632x operational requirement (100 samples/sec)

accuracy.note: Identical on both platforms (deterministic with seed=42)
accuracy.tpr_fpr_sweep: All thresholds: TPR=100%, FPR=0% (synthetic large-gap test)

miss_detection_latency.metric: Time to detect node crash after heartbeat stops
miss_detection_latency.note: Nearly identical -- dominated by sleep/check_interval timing, not CPU speed

throughput.metric: Log messages matched per second (regex, 10k messages)

jetson_vs_pc_ratio.note: Jetson achieves 53% of PC throughput for regex matching

cross_device_dds_latency.method: Round-trip time (RTT) measured on PC; echo node on Jetson

jetson_during_benchmark.note: Negligible impact from HELIX algorithmic benchmarks

e2e_ros2_latency.reason: helix_msgs package not built in any available workspace
e2e_ros2_latency.gap: Requires colcon build of helix_msgs to measure full ROS 2 callback chain latency

key_findings: Jetson throughput is 64% of PC for anomaly detection (63k vs 99k samples/sec)
Jetson throughput is 53% of PC for log parsing (155k vs 294k msg/sec)
Both exceed operational requirements by >600x (anomaly) and >1500x (log parsing)
Detection accuracy is identical on both platforms (deterministic algorithms)
Cross-device DDS latency is <1ms one-way over wired Ethernet
Jetson has 13GB free RAM and 82% idle CPU with GO2 stack running
HELIX benchmarks have negligible thermal/memory impact on Jetson
